title: axios npm Supply Chain Attack Executes Malicious Payload Only Inside Container Environments
description: A compromised axios npm package contained container-aware malicious code that detected Docker and Kubernetes runtime environments before executing its Remote Access Trojan payload, remaining dormant on standard developer workstations to evade detection.
label: AV-400

title: ReversingLabs Identifies Malicious PyPI Packages Installed Automatically via Dependency Automation Scripts
description: ReversingLabs researchers identified malicious PyPI packages exploiting automated dependency management scripts that automatically installed packages matching naming patterns, causing dependency bots and renovate-style tools to silently install typosquatted malware.
label: AV-200

title: Snyk Documents Dependency Confusion Injected During Multi-Stage Docker Container Builds
description: Snyk researchers documented dependency confusion attacks targeting intermediate stages of multi-stage Docker builds where malicious public packages were injected during build-only stages, compromising final production images while evading runtime security scanning.
label: AV-509

title: Checkmarx Documents Trojan Source Unicode Obfuscation Silently Overriding Configuration Values
description: Checkmarx researchers documented Trojan Source attacks where hidden Unicode characters silently modify configuration values in source code, causing security-critical settings such as authentication endpoints and encryption parameters to resolve to attacker-controlled values.
label: AV-300

title: Codecov Third-Party CI Integration Compromised to Exfiltrate Environment Credentials
description: Attackers compromised the Codecov bash uploader CI integration by modifying it to collect and exfiltrate environment variables including AWS credentials and tokens, affecting thousands of organizations that had integrated Codecov into their CI pipelines.
label: AV-410

title: Datadog Identifies Malicious macOS PyPI Package Performing Staged Credential Exfiltration Across Runs
description: Datadog Security Labs identified a malicious PyPI package targeting macOS developers that collected and exfiltrated credentials gradually across multiple execution runs, breaking the exfiltration into small chunks to evade network monitoring and anomaly detection.
label: AV-400

title: Alex Birsan Research Shows Typosquatting Combined With Dependency Confusion Amplifies Attack Reach
description: Alex Birsan's research demonstrated how combining typosquatting with dependency confusion creates a chained attack where a typosquatted package triggers further dependency confusion by introducing additional package name collisions in the victim's build environment.
label: AV-200

title: JFrog Documents Dependency Confusion via Docker Registry Mirror Overriding Private Internal Images
description: JFrog researchers documented dependency confusion attacks targeting Docker registry mirrors where public Docker Hub images with matching names automatically overrode private internal container images during build processes, injecting attacker-controlled containers.
label: AV-509

title: Snyk Documents Trojan Source Unicode Hidden Characters Altering CI Pipeline YAML Configuration
description: Snyk researchers documented Trojan Source attacks where hidden Unicode characters alter the structure and interpretation of CI pipeline YAML configuration files, causing build systems to execute different pipeline steps from what administrators see when reviewing configurations.
label: AV-300

title: GitHub Security Lab Documents Artifact Poisoning Attack Injecting Malicious Payload Between Pipeline Stages
description: GitHub Security Lab documented pwn-requests attacks where malicious build artifacts are injected between pipeline stages, causing later pipeline stages to download and execute compromised artifacts that contain attacker-controlled payloads.
label: AV-410

title: PyTorch-nightly Compromised Package Rewrites Its Own Files Post-Install to Insert Hidden Payload
description: The malicious torchtriton package that hijacked PyTorch-nightly builds used post-installation file modification to rewrite its own package files, inserting a hidden payload after installation completed to evade install-time security scanning.
label: AV-400

title: Socket.dev Research Identifies npm Packages That Ship Clean Versions Before Introducing Malicious Updates
description: Socket.dev security research identified a pattern of malicious npm packages that deliberately shipped multiple clean versions to establish trust and download history before introducing credential-stealing code in later updates, exploiting developer reliance on version history.
label: AV-200

title: OWASP Documents Dependency Confusion Resolved Only During CI Builds Due to Environment Configuration Differences
description: OWASP documented dependency confusion vulnerabilities where malicious packages are resolved exclusively during CI builds due to differences between CI and local development registry configurations, making the attack invisible to developers testing locally.
label: AV-509

title: JFrog Documents Trojan Source Unicode Characters Altering Dynamically Generated Code Behavior
description: JFrog researchers documented advanced Trojan Source attacks where hidden Unicode characters alter the behavior of dynamically generated code, causing code generation templates and meta-programming constructs to produce malicious output invisible during template review.
label: AV-300

title: Nx Build System GitHub Actions Pipeline Executes Malicious Payload Only After Multiple Successful Runs
description: The compromised Nx build system npm package contained a pipeline execution counter that activated its data-stealing payload only after a configured number of successful CI runs, significantly reducing detection probability during initial deployment and testing.
label: AV-410

title: dYdX Malicious Package Disables Security Validation Checks Before Executing Wallet-Stealing Payload
description: Threat actors published malicious dYdX client packages that disabled security validation and certificate checking mechanisms before executing their cryptocurrency wallet-stealing payload, preventing detection by security monitoring tools integrated into the development environment.
label: AV-400

title: Phylum Documents Malicious npm Package Promoted via Automated Dependency Update Tool Suggestions
description: Phylum researchers discovered malicious npm packages that were automatically suggested by dependency update tools such as Dependabot and Renovate due to typosquatted naming, causing automated pull requests to introduce malicious packages into projects without developer review.
label: AV-200

title: Unit42 Documents Dependency Confusion via Internal Package Names Leaked Through Docker Build Context
description: Palo Alto Networks Unit42 documented dependency confusion attacks where internal package names were exposed through Docker build context files and layer history, allowing attackers to register matching public packages and override private dependencies in containerized builds.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Examples Demonstrate Unicode Characters Altering JSON Configuration Parsing Behavior
description: The trojan-source examples repository demonstrates how hidden Unicode characters embedded in JSON configuration files alter how parsers interpret key-value pairs, causing security-critical configuration to be read differently by different JSON parsing implementations.
label: AV-300

title: Datadog Documents npm Supply Chain Attack via Compromised Specific Action Version Pinning
description: Datadog Security Labs documented supply chain attacks where specific pinned versions of GitHub Actions and npm packages were compromised after developers had pinned to them for stability, exploiting the false security assumption that pinned versions remain trustworthy.
label: AV-410

title: Malicious npm Package Delays Payload Execution Until System Reaches Idle State
description: A malicious npm package documented in the GitHub Advisory Database implemented system activity monitoring to delay payload execution until CPU and network activity dropped below threshold levels, triggering credential theft during idle periods to avoid behavioral detection.
label: AV-400

title: GitHub Advisory Database Documents Typosquatted Package Mimicking Enterprise Internal Library Naming Conventions
description: Multiple GitHub Security Advisories document malicious packages that mimic common enterprise internal library naming conventions such as company-utils or internal-auth, tricking developers into installing attacker-controlled packages they believe are internal tools.
label: AV-200

title: GitHub Advisory Database Documents Dependency Confusion Loading Malicious Package Only in Production
description: GitHub Security Advisories document dependency confusion vulnerabilities where registry configuration differences between development and production environments cause malicious public packages to be resolved exclusively in production deployments, making the attack invisible during local testing.
label: AV-509

title: Kaspersky Documents Trojan Source Unicode Characters Manipulating Feature Flag Logic
description: Kaspersky security researchers documented Trojan Source attacks where hidden Unicode characters alter feature flag evaluation logic, causing security-critical features such as multi-factor authentication and encryption to be silently disabled while appearing enabled in source code.
label: AV-300

title: Unit42 Shai-Hulud Attack Executes Malicious Payload via Poisoned npm Build Cache Artifacts
description: Palo Alto Networks Unit42 documented the Shai-Hulud npm supply chain attack where compromised cache artifacts were poisoned between build runs, causing subsequent pipeline executions to download and execute attacker-controlled payloads from the compromised build cache.
label: AV-410

title: ReversingLabs Documents Malicious Package Activating Payload Only Under Specific Timezone Conditions
description: ReversingLabs researchers documented obfuscated malicious packages that used timezone detection to activate payloads only when executed in specific geographic regions, targeting developers in particular countries while remaining dormant elsewhere to evade global security research.
label: AV-400

title: OSV Database Documents Typosquatted PyPI Package Introducing Additional Malicious Transitive Dependencies
description: The Open Source Vulnerability database documents malicious PyPI typosquatting packages that introduce additional malicious transitive dependencies upon installation, expanding the attack surface beyond the initial package and complicating complete remediation.
label: AV-200

title: OSV Database Documents Dependency Confusion via Package Alias Resolution Misconfiguration
description: The Open Source Vulnerability database documents dependency confusion attacks exploiting package alias and redirect configurations where incorrectly configured aliases resolve to malicious public packages instead of the intended private internal dependencies.
label: AV-509

title: Unit42 Documents Trojan Source Unicode Characters Modifying Script Shebang Interpreter Selection
description: Palo Alto Networks Unit42 documented Trojan Source attacks where hidden Unicode characters alter script shebang line interpretation, causing scripts to execute under a different interpreter than intended, enabling privilege escalation or security bypass through interpreter substitution.
label: AV-300

title: axios GitHub Actions Workflow Vulnerable to Staged Artifact Replacement Between Pipeline Stages
description: A vulnerability in the axios npm package GitHub Actions workflow allowed attackers to replace build artifacts between pipeline stages via pull_request_target exploitation, causing downstream stages to process and execute attacker-controlled content with elevated permissions.
label: AV-410

title: Checkmarx Identifies PyPI Supply Chain Attack Using Install Hook to Execute Hidden Malicious Scripts
description: Checkmarx researchers identified malicious PyPI packages that weaponized Python package install hooks including setup.py and pyproject.toml post-install scripts to execute hidden malicious commands with installer privileges during the pip installation process.
label: AV-400

title: axois Malicious npm Package Typosquats axios Library and Captures API Request Data
description: The malicious npm package axois used a character transposition typo to impersonate the popular axios HTTP client, intercepting and capturing all HTTP API requests and responses including authentication tokens and sensitive payload data sent through the compromised library.
label: AV-200

title: Socket.dev Documents Dependency Confusion via Misconfigured Private npm Scope Allowing Public Override
description: Socket.dev researchers documented dependency confusion attacks targeting incorrectly configured private npm scopes where missing or misconfigured scope registry mappings allowed public npm packages with matching scoped names to override private internal dependencies.
label: AV-509

title: BleepingComputer Documents Trojan Source Unicode Characters Hiding Authentication Bypass Logic
description: BleepingComputer documented Trojan Source attacks where hidden Unicode characters create invisible authentication bypass logic, causing login and authorization checks to silently grant access without proper credential validation while appearing to enforce security controls.
label: AV-300

title: Checkmarx Documents Poisoned Pipeline Execution via Download and Execution of Compromised Artifact
description: Checkmarx researchers documented Poisoned Pipeline Execution attacks where CI pipelines download and execute compromised build artifacts from attacker-controlled or compromised artifact repositories, enabling arbitrary code execution within trusted CI infrastructure.
label: AV-410

title: noblox.js-proxy-server Malicious Package Silently Monitors and Exfiltrates Developer Clipboard Data
description: The malicious noblox.js-proxy-server npm package implemented clipboard monitoring functionality that silently captured clipboard contents including API keys, passwords, and cryptocurrency wallet addresses, transmitting them to attacker-controlled infrastructure.
label: AV-400

title: Unit42 Documents Malicious PyPI Packages Faking Download Counts and Star Ratings for Credibility
description: Palo Alto Networks Unit42 documented sophisticated malicious PyPI packages that artificially inflated download counts and manipulated popularity metrics to appear as widely-used legitimate libraries, significantly increasing developer trust and installation rates.
label: AV-200

title: Phylum Documents Dependency Confusion via Incorrect Registry Priority Order in Package Manager Configuration
description: Phylum researchers documented dependency confusion attacks exploiting incorrect registry priority ordering in package manager configurations where public registries were inadvertently assigned higher priority than private registries, causing malicious public packages to consistently override internal dependencies.
label: AV-509

title: PHP CodeSniffer Extra Documents Trojan Source Unicode Altering Loop Termination Condition
description: The PHPCSExtra project documented Trojan Source vulnerabilities in PHP code where hidden Unicode characters alter loop termination conditions, causing infinite loops or premature loop exits that bypass security controls appearing as proper bounded iterations during code review.
label: AV-300

title: Snyk Documents GitHub Actions Poisoned Pipeline via Malicious Command Injected Through Environment Variables
description: Snyk researchers documented GitHub Actions pipeline poisoning where malicious commands were injected through manipulated environment variables, enabling arbitrary command execution within CI workflow steps that consumed environment variables without proper sanitization.
label: AV-410

title: tj-actions Supply Chain Compromise Delivers Malicious Docker Image Running Hidden Background Process
description: The compromised tj-actions/changed-files GitHub Action delivered a malicious Docker container image that launched hidden background processes consuming system resources and exfiltrating CI environment data while the foreground action appeared to function normally.
label: AV-400

title: lodahs Malicious npm Package Typosquats lodash Library and Executes Payload on Install
description: The malicious npm package lodahs used a character substitution typosquatting technique to impersonate the extremely popular lodash utility library, executing credential-stealing code during installation that targeted the millions of projects using lodash as a dependency.
label: AV-200

title: ReversingLabs Documents Dependency Confusion via Fallback Mirror Registry Resolving Malicious Package
description: ReversingLabs researchers documented dependency confusion attacks exploiting package manager fallback behavior where mirror registry unavailability caused automatic resolution to public registries hosting malicious packages with matching internal dependency names.
label: AV-509

title: OCaml Community Documents Trojan Source Unicode Hiding Conditional Execution Branch in Parser Logic
description: The OCaml community documented Trojan Source vulnerabilities in OCaml parser implementations where hidden Unicode bidirectional characters create invisible conditional execution branches, causing the compiler to generate different machine code from what the source appears to specify.
label: AV-300

title: StepSecurity Documents Nx Build System CI Pipeline Compromised via Untrusted External Script Execution
description: StepSecurity documented the Nx build system compromise where the CI pipeline executed scripts fetched from an untrusted external source, allowing attackers to inject data-stealing malware into the build process of thousands of enterprise development teams.
label: AV-410

title: Datadog Documents npm Packages Collecting and Exfiltrating OS and Hardware System Metadata
description: Datadog Security Labs documented malicious npm packages that systematically collected operating system details, hardware fingerprints, network configuration, and installed software inventories, transmitting comprehensive system profiles to attacker infrastructure for targeted follow-up attacks.
label: AV-400

title: PyPI Typosquatting Package Uses Higher Version Number to Supersede Legitimate requests Library
description: A malicious PyPI package targeting the requests library published itself at a significantly higher version number than the legitimate package, exploiting package manager version resolution to automatically supersede the legitimate library in environments using flexible version specifiers.
label: AV-200

title: SecurityWeek Reports Dependency Confusion Targeting Development Dependencies at Apple Microsoft and Others
description: SecurityWeek reported on dependency confusion attacks specifically targeting development-only dependencies at Apple, Microsoft, and other major technology companies, demonstrating that devDependencies and build tools are equally vulnerable to confusion attacks as production dependencies.
label: AV-509

title: NVD CVE-2021-42574 Documents Trojan Source Unicode Characters Altering String Comparison Equality Checks
description: The National Vulnerability Database documents CVE-2021-42574 where invisible Unicode bidirectional control characters alter string comparison and equality check logic, causing security-critical comparisons such as password validation and token verification to produce incorrect results.
label: AV-300

title: GitHub Security Lab Documents CI Pipeline Executing Base64-Encoded Malicious Payload via Environment Variable Decoding
description: GitHub Security Lab documented pipeline poisoning attacks where base64-encoded malicious payloads stored in workflow environment variables are decoded and executed at runtime, bypassing static workflow analysis tools that cannot evaluate dynamically decoded command strings.
label: AV-410
